disclaimer: Educational assessment. CARSI is an IICRC-approved CEC provider; this is CARSI-original Australian training, not IICRC certification.
note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Answer keys independently verified against the course text. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Commercial Floor Care for Schools and Childcare Contracts - Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: Under the model WHS framework, how often must a Safety Data Sheet be reviewed for it to be considered current?
options: Every 12 months
At least every 5 years
Only when the product changes
Every 10 years
questions.sourceRef: Module 1 - Your WHS duties: current SDS, the chemicals register and labelling

questions.questionText: Which statement about Victoria is correct regarding chemical safety law?
options: Victoria has not adopted any chemical safety laws
Victoria operates under its own OHS Act and Regulations rather than the model WHS framework
Victoria's rules are identical to the model WHS Regulations in every respect
Victoria does not require Safety Data Sheets
questions.sourceRef: Module 1 - Your WHS duties: current SDS, the chemicals register and labelling

questions.questionText: You decant a floor stripper from its drum into a spray bottle for the day's work. What must you do?
options: Nothing - only the original drum needs a label
Correctly label the decanted spray bottle as well
Add it to a manifest
Dispose of it the same day so no label is needed
questions.sourceRef: Module 1 - Your WHS duties: current SDS, the chemicals register and labelling

questions.questionText: Which Quality Area of the National Quality Standard is the main reason for choosing child-safe, low-tox products and controlling infection in childcare?
options: Quality Area 1 (Educational program and practice)
Quality Area 2 (Children's health and safety)
Quality Area 5 (Relationships with children)
Quality Area 7 (Governance and leadership)
questions.sourceRef: Module 1 - Choosing child-safe, low-tox products and ventilating occupied rooms

questions.questionText: What best describes the status of ANSI/IICRC S100 for carpet cleaning?
options: A government regulation that legally mandates how carpet must be cleaned
A recognised industry standard of care, not law, that supports your judgement
A SafeWork Australia code of practice
A state-based licensing requirement for cleaners
questions.sourceRef: Module 2 - Carpet, fibre and soil: an overview

questions.questionText: Compared with encapsulation, hot water extraction (HWE) is generally associated with:
options: Deeper soil removal but more moisture and a longer return to service
The lowest moisture and the fastest return to service
No need to vacuum beforehand
A guaranteed fixed dry time
questions.sourceRef: Module 2 - Hot water extraction vs encapsulation: the dry-time trade-off

questions.questionText: Why do grout lines tend to look dingy even when the tiles look clean?
options: Grout is always a darker colour than tile
Grout is porous and traps soil below the surface, while the denser tile face rinses clean
Tiles are never sealed and grout always is
Mopping cleans grout more effectively than tile
questions.sourceRef: Module 3 - Hard-floor and grout soil: tools, brushes and chemistry

questions.questionText: In the generic strip-and-seal process, what is the correct order of stages?
options: Apply finish, then strip, then rinse
Strip old finish, rinse and neutralise, dry, then apply multiple thin sealer and finish coats with drying between coats
Dry, strip, apply one thick coat, reopen
Neutralise, apply finish, strip, rinse
questions.sourceRef: Module 4 - The strip-and-seal process, end to end

questions.questionText: Where do the correct dry, cure and re-entry times for a sealer or finish come from?
options: A fixed industry rule you can memorise
The product's SDS and TDS
Whatever the previous job used
questions.sourceRef: Module 4 - Sealing and finishing: cure and re-entry come from the SDS/TDS

questions.questionText: Which standard is the correct basis to verify slip resistance of a finished, in-service floor after sealing?
options: AS 4586:2013, which classifies new surfaces before installation
AS 4663:2013, which measures existing surfaces in service
Neither - slip resistance cannot be measured
The SDS, which sets the slip class
questions.sourceRef: Module 4 - Verifying slip resistance to a target class (AS 4663)

questions.questionText: Before discharging pressure-washing wash water (trade wastewater) to the sewer, you must:
options: Dilute it with clean water until it looks clear
Have prior written approval - a trade-waste agreement - from the water authority
Notify the school after the job is done
Nothing - the sewer accepts any liquid waste
questions.sourceRef: Module 5 - The drain is just for rain: capturing run-off and trade-waste to sewer

questions.questionText: Working With Children checks in Australia are:
options: A single national check valid everywhere
Issued only to permanent school staff, never contractors
State and territory based, generally not auto-transferable, and not necessarily the only screening a site requires
Replaced entirely by a police check
questions.sourceRef: Module 6 - Compliance and documentation: SDS register, WWC checks, NQS QA2 and school cleaning standards
